methods.description: Process for cleaning foraminiferal shells to remove organic matter and prepare them for further analysis, following an adapted version of Barker et al. (2003).

steps.description: All foraminifera were transferred to 10 mL polyethylene vials.
To each vial, 10 mL of 1% H2O2 solution (buffered with 0.5 M NH4OH) was added to remove organic matter.
steps.searchable_summary: add H2O2 solution to vials to remove organic matter

steps.description: The vials were heated for 10 min in a water bath at 95°C.
steps.searchable_summary: heat vials in water bath at 95°C for 10 minutes

steps.description: Vials were placed in an ultrasonic bath for 30 s (degas mode, 80 kHz, 50% power).
steps.searchable_summary: place vials in ultrasonic bath for 30 seconds

steps.description: These steps (organic removal procedure) were repeated five times.
steps.searchable_summary: repeat organic removal procedure five times

steps.description: Foraminiferal samples were rinsed five times with ultrapure water.
steps.searchable_summary: rinse foraminiferal samples with ultrapure water five times

steps.description: Vials were stored overnight in a laminar flow cabinet at room temperature to dry.
steps.searchable_summary: store vials in laminar flow cabinet overnight to dry

steps.description: Dried foraminifera were placed on double-sided tape on LA-ICP-MS stubs.
steps.searchable_summary: place dried foraminifera on LA-ICP-MS stubs

steps.description: Pictures were taken of individual foraminifera with a ZEISS Axioplan 2 fluorescence microscope equipped with appropriate excitation and emission optics and a ZEISS Axiocam MRc 5 camera to assess the number of chambers added during the experiment based on the incorporation of calcein.
steps.searchable_summary: take pictures of foraminifera with fluorescence microscope
